entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Students read about the story of cartography from ancient Mesopotamia up through the discovery of Antarctica in the late 1800s and draw maps along the way. The course finishes with a term of historical geography where students follow the steps of Canadian explorers from the 19th century and learn about Canadian provinces through map work.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: The first two terms are the same every year and shared between both US and Canadian courses. Term 3 brings in resources from the historical time period and is specific to Canadian students.

rows.label: Geography: Grade 7 (Canada)
rows.books: Mapping the World with Art, Canadian Student Atlas, Exploring the Frozen North
